data.title: DBV stock rallies post-market on FDA Viaskin update
data.description:
• DBV Technologies (NASDAQ:DBVT) stock shot up 29% in post-market trading Wednesday after the company said it has aligned with the FDA on an accelerated approval pathway for its peanut allergy patch Viaskin for children aged one to three years.
  • The French drug developer said the FDA has confirmed that efficacy data from its Phase 3 EPITOPE study can serve as an intermediate clinical endpoint to support accelerated approval, according to a statement.
  • The company is also planning to initiate a Phase 3 supplemental safety study called COMFORT Toddlers in Q2 2025 to support approval. DBV (DBVT) expects to submit a Biologics License Application for Viaskin Peanut patch for toddlers in the second half of 2026.
  • The FDA and DBV (DBVT) have also agreed on key design elements for a post-marketing confirmatory study for the product. The study is expected to be initiated around the time the BLA is submitted.

data.title: Telomir Pharmaceuticals enters into stock purchase agreement
data.description:
• Telomir Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:TELO) said it has entered into a stock purchase agreement with a certain accredited investor, pursuant to which the company issued 142,857 shares of common stock at a price of $7 per share for a total of $1 million.
  • The transaction did not involve the issuance of any warrants.
  • Filing

data.title: ECD Automotive Design announces receipt of Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• ECD Automotive Design (NASDAQ:ECDA) received a notice from Nasdaq stating that the company no longer meets Nasdaq Listing Rules due to its failure to file the Form 10-Q for the quarter ended September 30, 2024.
  • The company shall have 60 calendar days to submit a plan to regain compliance with the listing rule, and if Nasdaq accepts the company’s plan, or until May 19, 2025, to regain compliance with the listing rule.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BAE Systems wins $656M U.S. Army contract
data.description:
• BAE Systems (OTCPK:BAESF) has been awarded a $656.2M fixed-price-incentive contract from the U.S. Army to produce M2A4 and M7A4 vehicles.
  • Work locations and funding will be determined with each order, with an estimated completion date of Nov. 30, 2027.
  • Army Contracting Command, Detroit Arsenal, Michigan, is the contracting activity.

data.title: Editas cut to equal weight by Wells Fargo over reni-cel positioning
data.description:
Wells Fargo has downgraded Editas Medicine (NASDAQ:EDIT) to equal weight from overweight, citing potential competition for its therapy candidate reni-cel.

The investment bank said that while reni-cel’s Hb data at the American Society of Hematology conference was “consistent with prior readouts,” early data from rival Beam (BEAM) demonstrated better HbF induction and engraftment time “which may impact reni-cel’s positioning.”

Wells said that while the company’s sickle cell disease program could still be a “leader in the space and unlock major value,” it was still early stage.

“We await further preclinical proof-of-concept in LNP delivery and also look to understand how payload differences may translate in the in vivo landscape,” the bank added.

Wells lowered its price target for the stock to $4 from $7.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Nordson FY25 outlook falls below expectations, while FQ4 tops estimates
Mid-America Apartment Communities announces pricing of senior unsecured notes offering
data.description:
• Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA) announced that its operating partnership, Mid-America Apartments, L.P. priced a $350M offering of its's 4.9% senior unsecured notes due March 1, 2035, under its existing shelf registration statement.
  • The notes were priced at 99.170% of the principal amount.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on December 18, 2024.
  • Mid-America Apartments, L.P. intends to use net proceeds from the offering to repay borrowings outstanding under its unsecured commercial paper program, with any remaining net proceeds to be used for general corporate purposes, which may include, the repayment of other debt and the acquisition, development and redevelopment of apartment communities.
  • Source: Press Release
